heading.en: Things are nouns — add -s for plural

body.en: Name things with nouns. Put a or the before them. Make most plurals by adding -s (dog -> dogs). With 600 thing words plus a/the and -s, you can name the world.
body.zh: 用名词指称事物，前面加 a 或 the。大多数复数加 -s（dog→dogs）。用 600 个“事物”词，加上 a/the 和 -s，就能命名整个世界。

gloss.en: a hard shiny material like iron or gold

words.example: This box is made of metal.

gloss.en: the center, between the two ends

words.example: Pens are kept in the middle drawer.

gloss.en: the white drink that comes from cows

words.example: I drink a glass of milk every day.

gloss.en: the part of you that thinks and feels

words.example: Keep her words in your mind.

gloss.en: a deep hole in the ground for getting coal or gold

words.example: They dig for gold in the mine.

gloss.en: a unit of time, sixty seconds

words.example: Wait one minute, please.

gloss.en: thin cloud near the ground that is hard to see through

words.example: The hills were hidden by mist.

gloss.en: coins and paper you use to buy things

words.example: I need money to buy food.

gloss.en: one of the twelve parts of a year

words.example: July is a hot month.

gloss.en: the early part of the day

words.example: They left early this morning.

gloss.en: a female parent

words.example: I want to buy a present for my mother.

gloss.en: the act of moving

words.example: The motion of the boat made me sick.

gloss.en: a very high hill

words.example: We climbed up the high mountain.

gloss.en: a change of place or position

words.example: Don't make a move!

gloss.en: sounds that are nice to listen to

words.example: I like pop music.

gloss.en: a country and its people

words.example: China is a big nation.

gloss.en: something that you must have or do

words.example: There is a need for more food.

gloss.en: new information about what just happened

words.example: What's the latest news?

gloss.en: the dark time when people sleep

words.example: These animals only come out at night.

gloss.en: a sound, often loud or unpleasant

words.example: The car made a strange noise.

gloss.en: a short piece of writing to help you remember

words.example: Please make a note of the dates.

gloss.en: a word or sign that shows how many

words.example: Think of a number and double it.

gloss.en: the act of watching something with care

words.example: Careful observation of the birds taught us a lot.

gloss.en: saying you will give or do something

words.example: She got a job offer.

gloss.en: a thick liquid from underground or used in cooking

words.example: They drill for oil in the sea.

gloss.en: cutting open the body to fix a part

words.example: Will I need to have an operation?

gloss.en: what you think about something

words.example: In my opinion, the film was good.

gloss.en: the way things are placed one after another

words.example: The names are listed in order.

gloss.en: a group of people working for one aim

words.example: He works for a large organization.

gloss.en: a small pretty object that makes a place look nice

words.example: She put a small ornament on the shelf.

gloss.en: a person who owns something

words.example: He is the owner of the dog.

gloss.en: one side of a sheet in a book

words.example: Turn to page 64.

gloss.en: the bad feeling when your body is hurt

words.example: I felt a sharp pain in my leg.

gloss.en: a colored liquid put on surfaces

words.example: The wall has white paint.

gloss.en: the thin material you write on

words.example: I need a piece of paper.

gloss.en: some but not all of a thing

words.example: We spent part of the day in the park.

gloss.en: a soft wet mixture that can stick or spread

words.example: Mix the powder with water to make a paste.

gloss.en: the act of paying money

words.example: What method of payment do you prefer?

gloss.en: a time with no war or fighting

words.example: The whole world wants peace.

gloss.en: one human being

words.example: She is a kind person.

gloss.en: a point or area where something is

words.example: Keep your purse in a safe place.

gloss.en: a living thing that grows in the earth

words.example: All plants need light and water.

gloss.en: a story acted on a stage

words.example: We saw a play at the theater.

gloss.en: a happy or glad feeling

words.example: His eyes lit up with pleasure.

gloss.en: a thing you say to give your idea

words.example: She made a good point.

gloss.en: a substance that harms or kills the body

words.example: Some mushrooms contain a deadly poison.

gloss.en: a substance rubbed on something to make it shine

words.example: He put polish on his shoes.

gloss.en: a person whose job is to carry bags

words.example: The porter carried our bags to the room.

gloss.en: the place where something is

words.example: What is the best position for the lights?

gloss.en: a dry mass of very small fine grains

words.example: Add some milk powder to the cup.

gloss.en: the ability to control people or things

words.example: He has the power to change things.

gloss.en: the money you pay for something

words.example: The price of the book is ten dollars.

gloss.en: letters and words made by a machine on paper

words.example: The small print was hard to read.

gloss.en: a set of steps done to get a result

words.example: Making bread is a slow process.

gloss.en: things grown on a farm, like fruit

words.example: The market is full of farm produce.

gloss.en: the money you make after paying costs

words.example: The shop made a good profit this year.

gloss.en: things that belong to somebody

words.example: This bag is my property.

gloss.en: normal written language, not poetry

words.example: He writes in clear, simple prose.

gloss.en: an action that shows you are against something

words.example: They held a protest in the street.

gloss.en: the act of moving something toward you

words.example: I gave the door a sharp pull.

gloss.en: a way of making someone suffer for doing wrong

words.example: No food was his punishment.

gloss.en: the reason or aim of something

words.example: The purpose of the trip is to learn.

gloss.en: the act of moving something away from you

words.example: She gave him a gentle push.

gloss.en: how good or bad something is

words.example: This shop sells food of high quality.

gloss.en: words that ask for information

words.example: You didn't answer my question.

gloss.en: water that falls from the sky in drops

words.example: There will be rain tomorrow.

gloss.en: a variety of things of one type

words.example: The hotel offers a wide range of food.

gloss.en: how fast or how often something happens

words.example: Prices are rising at a fast rate.

gloss.en: a narrow line of light or heat

words.example: A ray of sun shone on the grass.

gloss.en: what you do because of something that happened

words.example: His reaction was to laugh.

gloss.en: the act of looking at and understanding words

words.example: My hobbies include reading.

gloss.en: why something happens or is done

words.example: He said no but gave no reason.

gloss.en: written notes kept to use later

words.example: You should keep a record of your money.

gloss.en: a sad feeling about something you did

words.example: She felt deep regret for her words.

gloss.en: the way people or groups deal with each other

words.example: The two countries have a good relation.

gloss.en: belief in a god and the worship of it

words.example: He never talks about his religion.

gloss.en: a person who speaks or acts for a group

words.example: She is the representative of our class.

gloss.en: the act of asking for something politely

words.example: I sent a request for help.

gloss.en: a strong good feeling for someone you admire

words.example: I have great respect for her work.

gloss.en: the part that is left

words.example: Take what you want and leave the rest.

gloss.en: something good given for hard work or help

words.example: She got a reward for finding the dog.

gloss.en: a regular pattern of sounds or beats

words.example: They danced to the rhythm of the music.

gloss.en: small white grains grown for food

words.example: We eat rice every day.

gloss.en: a long flow of water across the land

words.example: We swam in the cold river.

gloss.en: a hard path for cars to travel on

words.example: There are many cars on the road.

gloss.en: a small round loaf of bread

words.example: I ate soup and a roll.

gloss.en: a part of a building with walls and a floor

words.example: I heard him enter the room.

gloss.en: the act of moving your hand over a surface

words.example: Give the table a quick rub.

gloss.en: a statement of what you must or must not do

words.example: It is a rule that we stay quiet.

gloss.en: a period of moving fast on your feet

words.example: I go for a run every morning.

gloss.en: a white substance added to food for taste

words.example: Pass the salt, please.

gloss.en: very small grains of rock on a beach

words.example: The children played in the sand.

gloss.en: the size of something compared to others

words.example: They built the house on a large scale.

gloss.en: the study of the natural world by facts

words.example: I like to learn about science.

gloss.en: the large body of salt water

words.example: We swam in the warm sea.

gloss.en: a place where you can sit

words.example: She sat back in her seat.

gloss.en: a person who works in an office, helping with mail and calls

words.example: The secretary answered the phone.

gloss.en: the act of choosing from a group

words.example: She took a long time to make her selection.

gloss.en: the kind of person you really are

words.example: You'll soon feel your old self again.

gloss.en: an understanding or ability to judge something

words.example: She has a good sense of humour.
